items.briefing:
Australian organisations are facing a shifting cyber threat landscape. While overall cybercrime risk for Australians has reportedly eased slightly, small and medium businesses (SMBs) are increasingly being targeted — making staff awareness more important than ever. Separately, a global study found most organisations have already experienced AI-related security incidents, a growing concern as AI tools become more common in workplaces like ours. Critical software vulnerabilities are also doubling year-on-year, meaning unpatched systems pose a serious risk.

**Practical tip:** Make sure your devices and software are set to update automatically — patching known vulnerabilities remains one of the simplest and most effective ways to stay protected.

featured.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
featured.quote: Cybercrime risk shifting from large institutions to vulnerable Australian SMBs lacking resources.
